programme.title: Our Dream Christmas
programme.desc: With Gabby's mother dying, her family wants to spend one last Christmas together. Over the course of two days, Gabby uncovers the truth about her husband, defends herself against her sick, judgmental mom and faces a potential job loss.

programme.title: The Pledge
programme.desc: A retiring police detective pledges to catch the killer of a young child.

programme.title: Pacific Heights

programme.title: Limitless

programme.title: Legionnaire

programme.title: The Marine 6: Close Quarters
programme.desc: Jake Carter and another former Marine, Luke Trapper, join forces to rescue a kidnapped girl from a gang of international criminals.Continuation of the franchise. (Edited for Television)

programme.title: Company Of Heroes
programme.desc: Set during World War II, follows US military units during the Battle of Normandy and the Allied liberation of France.

programme.title: The Beast
programme.desc: Spectacular action sequences punctuate an emotionally charged war story that pits Soviet soldiers against Afghan rebels.

programme.title: The China Syndrome
programme.desc: A reporter finds what appears to be a cover-up of safety hazards at a nuclear power plant.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: The true story of Joy Adamson and her husband, George, who adopt a slain lioness' cubs.

programme.title: The Three Stooges In Orbit
programme.desc: The Three Stooges are tangling with Martians.

programme.title: You Can't Take It With You
programme.desc: Lionel Barrymore is the eccentric patriarch of a clan of frustrated artists who decided 30 years earlier to retire from the rat race and use his fortune to encourage friends and family to pursue vocations that really interest them. At the center of his family is his granddaughter, Jean Arthur, who is carrying on a romance with her boss son, James Stewart.

programme.title: Mr. Deeds Goes To Town
programme.desc: A reluctant millionaire (Gary Cooper) is declared insane when he announces that he ll give his fortune away to America s poor. Oscar winning direction by Frank Capra.

programme.title: The Gunman
programme.desc: From the director of Taken comes The Gunman, a pulse-pounding action thriller starring Sean Penn. It's been eight years since Jim Terrier (Sean Penn) worked as a black ops assassin and now someone from his old organization wants him dead. Pursued across Africa and Europe by elite hit men, Terrier is dragged back into his dangerous past as he engages in a relentless cat-and-mouse game to take out the man who betrayed him.

programme.title: Abduction
programme.desc: Quinn (Scott Adkins, Doctor Strange), a member of a SWAT unit, steps out of a park fountain in an Asian city with no recollection of who he is or where he came from. As he pieces together clues from his past, he vaguely recalls his young daughter, who has been kidnapped. Meanwhile, Conner (Andy On, Zombie Fight Club), a former military operative turned gangster-for-hire, discovers that his wife has also disappeared mysteriously in the middle of the night. These two men, with little in common, realize they must work together to find their loved ones and thwart their mysterious abductors.

programme.title: Red Headed Stranger
programme.desc: Revisionist western about fallen preacher Shay, who guns down his wife Raysha for running off with another man. Wandering, he meets single mom Laurie. However, helpless sheriff Scoby wants Shay to help him fight the villainous Clavers

programme.title: Learning To Drive
programme.desc: As her marriage dissolves, a Manhattan writer takes driving lessons from a Sikh instructor with marriage troubles of his own. In each other's company they find the courage to get back on the road and the strength to take the wheel.

programme.title: City Hunter [Dubbed]
programme.desc: Jackie Chan stars as P.I. Ryu Saeba, who's been hired to find the runaway daughter of a publishing tycoon. Ryu's first impulse is to turn the job downâ€¦until he sees a picture of the beautiful girl, Shizuko.

programme.title: Set Up
programme.desc: After a heist, a man is betrayed and vows to get revenge, joining forces with a ruthless crime lord.

programme.title: Replicant
programme.desc: In order to destroy the future's most ruthless killer, they had to create him.

programme.title: Ticker
programme.desc: After a detective's partner is murdered by Northern Irish terrorists, the officer joins the bomb squad.

programme.title: The Condemned
programme.desc: Nine criminals are placed on an island. Only the last person alive in 30 hours leaves the island.

programme.title: The Condemned 2
programme.desc: In the revamped Condemned Tournament, convicts must fight to the death in a game broadcast to the public.

programme.title: Taken From Rio Bravo
programme.desc: Two friends pursue a group of human traffickers that have kidnapped five women in 1874, East Texas.

programme.title: A Witness to Murder
programme.desc: Sanitation worker MaryBeth is in the wrong place at the wrong time when the mayor's wife is murdered. She refuses to talk to the sheriff she dislikes, but the killers know she was there. Now they're coming for her to make sure she stays silent.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Her Deadly Game
programme.desc: When her childhood friend Kayla transfers to her college basketball team, Andrea's reunion turns into a nightmare. Kayla, obsessed with being Andrea's one and only best friend, will harm anyone who gets in her way.

programme.title: Salvation
programme.desc: A foster child bonds with a snake-handling Pentecostal community but uncovers sinister truths. She must choose between reporting pastor's crimes or preserving her newfound support system, risking personal safety for a sense of belonging.

programme.title: Wolf Pack
programme.desc: A physician is forced to join an elite mercenary unit in the hopes of uncovering the truth about his father's death. Soon, the group discovers a dangerous conspiracy that can threaten the lives of millions.

programme.title: Ip Man
programme.desc: The life story of Yip Man, the first person to teach the Chinese martial art of Wing Chun.

programme.title: Ip Man 2
programme.desc: Master Ip, a new arrival in British-occupied Hong Kong, wants to open a martial arts academy to teach his unique Wing Chun style. A corrupt group of martial arts masters, led by Master Hung refuses to allow him to teach in peace until he proves himself as a kung fu master.

programme.title: Ip Man 3

programme.title: Secret Life of the Surgeon's Wife
programme.desc: Julia suspects her dental surgeon husband is having an affair.

programme.title: Cellmate Secrets
programme.desc: Smith's former cellmates discuss her surprising antics behind bars.

programme.title: Secrets in the Foundation
programme.desc: Elena, her daughter and dad move into a new house for a fresh start.

programme.title: Secrets to Kill For
programme.desc: Shanice joins the search for her childhood friend who is missing.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Surgeon's Wife
programme.desc: Julia suspects her dental surgeon husband is having an affair.

programme.title: Love's Fast Lane
programme.desc: Nikki, a mechanic, falls for publicist Jeremy as they aim to make her the first female car show host.

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks
programme.desc: At the Gamma Christmas Mixer, Sarah is missing and her friends' search leads to a shocking attack.

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks
programme.desc: Most leave for break, while a few stay behind. Rick is pushed to his breaking point and flips out on Allison.

programme.title: Let Love Grow
programme.desc: Landscape architect Evie meets her match with new client Ryan, whose daughters are her biggest fan.

programme.title: Best Friend From Heaven
programme.desc: When her dog is killed, Tara cancels her wedding. But her dog's ghost and her small town have a surprise.

programme.title: Cradle of Lies
programme.desc: Marissa was in a coma while pregnant and wakes up five years later to find that the baby she delivered is gone

programme.title: The Bouquet
programme.desc: Two estranged sisters set aside their differences to help their mother save her floral business.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Zola
programme.desc: Zola, a Detroit waitress, is seduced into a weekend of partying in Florida. Her trip turns into a wild saga involving a pimp, an idiot boyfriend, and Tampa's finest strip clubs in this film based on the greatest Twitter story ever told.

programme.title: Traffickers
programme.desc: Tiffany Cooper, disgraced by the military, wanted by the cops and running out of time, stops at nothing to take down a Trafficking Operation and save these young girls. Nothing is what it seems when everyone seems to be against you. Trust No One.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: Murder Without Motive
programme.desc: Edmund Perry has everything going for him after he graduates with a scholarship to attend Stanford. With his dedication paying off, an encounter with a police officer senselessly ends his life.

programme.title: The Code
programme.desc: A veteran thief recruits a younger crook to help him pull off one final job in order to repay his debt to the Russian mob.

programme.title: Crush
programme.desc: Haunted by her past, a young woman's life is complicated by love, self-discovery, and surrounding violence.

programme.title: Quiet as Kept
programme.desc: Jay is unlawfully detained. His ex-girlfriend returns, and his business partners work to clear his name. Meanwhile, a crooked cop is under suspicion for murder, and connections are made between him and Jay. Torn between duty and loyalty, Detective Johnson is caught in the middle. As the prosecution struggles, Jay and his business partners work to exist the dangerous world cleanly.

programme.title: Counteract
programme.desc: Amateur and immature social media influencer, Sylvia Shaw has set out to aide her struggling family from financial ruin. Doing so, she is lured into working for a mysterious company that promises fame and great wealth. But soon realizes, her new employer is a mystery, for many reasons.

programme.title: Carmen and Magic
programme.desc: When Carmen meets a new friend at school named Magic, she helps raise her spirits and allows her to stand up for herself. When her single father Drew starts seeing the school principal, Magic convinces Carmen to disrupt their relationship, pulling the veil on Magic’s sinister nature.

programme.title: You Made Me This Way
programme.desc: Haunted by a failed assassination attempt, ruthless drug lord Kwame becomes obsessed with Aubrey, the woman who nearly ended his life. Consumed by revenge, he kidnaps her, setting in motion a deadly game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: Zoombies
programme.desc: A virus spreads through a safari park and turns the animals undead. Those in the park must stop the creatures.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Giant Octopus
programme.desc: The California coast is terrorized by two enormous prehistoric sea creatures.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Crocosaurus
programme.desc: Surviving the last battle, a massive prehistoric shark is threatened by a new foe discovered in the jungle.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Giant Octopus
programme.desc: The California coast is terrorized by two enormous prehistoric sea creatures.

programme.title: Megaboa
programme.desc: College students look for cave drawings in Columbia, but find a once-extinct and deadly 50-foot boa constrictor.

programme.title: Aenigma (Edited Version)
programme.desc: For his final horror hit of the '80s, writer/director Lucio Fulci combined elements of CARRIE, PHENOMENA and SUSPIRIA with the grisly surrealism of his own past classics for one last shocker packed with brooding, ominous imagery and signature Fulci style (The Horror Syndicate): When a bullied student at a New England girls school becomes comatose after a prank gone wrong, her tormenters will suffer graphic telepathic punishment that includes the infamous 'death by snails' scene. Jared Martin (DALLAS), Lara Lamberti (A BLADE IN THE DARK) and Ulli Reinthaler (ZOMBIE 3) star in this blast from start to finish, with some of the most bonkers imagery Fulci ever put on film (The Video Vacuum) co-written by Giorgio Mariuzzo (THE BEYOND, HOUSE BY THE CEMETERY).

programme.title: The Loch Ness Monster
programme.desc: In the arena of cryptozoology, few creatures have captured the imagination like the Loch Ness Monster. Sightings and stories of this beast of lore go back decades. Also known as Nessie for short, this creature is said to occupy Loch Ness in the Scottish Highlands and is often described as massive and long-necked. Widespread interest and belief in the Monster have increased significantly since it was brought to worldwide attention in 1933. Evidence of its presence is anecdotal, with several disputed photos and sonar examinations, but does the Loch Ness Monster really exist? Follow us as we deep dive into the facts.

programme.title: Castle of the Creeping Flesh
programme.desc: In an ancient castle, a mad scientist is trying to revive his dead daughter by way of an operation. However, there are certain body parts he needs that he is unable to get. Fortunately for the scientist, his problem is solved when a group of unfortunate and drunken party-goers stumble into his castle.

programme.title: Maniac Cop
programme.desc: A killer dressed in a police uniform begins murdering innocent people on the streets of New York City.

programme.title: BODY BAGS (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Body Bags is an anthology horror film made up of three stories bookended by a creepy morgue assistant played by John Carpenter himself. The first story, The Gas Station, features Robert Carradine as a serial killer, with cameos by Sam Raimi and Wes Craven. Hair follows Stacy Keach as he receives a botched hair transplant that infests him with an alien parasite, and Eye is another transplant story, this time featuring Mark Hamill as a baseball player who loses an eye in a car accident and receives a transplant, only to be overtaken with the personality of the eye's previous owner: a murderous misogynist.

programme.title: A Dark Song
programme.desc: A heartbroken woman and recluse occultist perform an ancient, dark ritual to bring her child back to life - but missteps have consequences and unknown evils are unleashed.

programme.title: The Nightshifter
programme.desc: Stênio works the graveyard shift in the morgue of a vast, violent city. Each night he prepares the lifeless corpses for examination, but he's never alone. For Stênio can talk to the dead...

programme.title: Housemaid, The (2018)
programme.desc: On a plantation in 1953 Indochina, a troubled young housemaid finds comfort in the arms of the widowed owner, until his deceased wife returns to object.

programme.title: Consecration
programme.desc: After her brother dies under strange circumstances, Grace travels to the Mount Saviour Convent for answers. Once there, she uncovers murder, sacrilege, and a shocking truth about her past.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: A great animal attack movie pushes all of our human-against-nature buttons. From demented sharks to unhinged chimps, this episode sorts out the leaders of the cinematic pack. We also shame the worst horror pets (and give treats to the best).

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Are Overdrawn" - The Clampetts receive an overdraft from the bank intended for the deadbeat actor J.D. 'Jake' Clampett, while he gets their statement and starts to spend their money.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampette Look" - Cynthia Fenwick makes her filthy rich mother dress in the "Clampett Look" when they visit the Clampetts, since she thinks Elly is a fashion maven. That only reinforces the Clampett's belief that the Fenwicks are poor.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. programme.title: Springtime in the Sierras
programme.desc: Ads for this Roy Rogers classic roared HEAD FOR THE MUSICAL HILLS...ROY IS GUNNIN' FOR THRILLS! And thrills are what you'll get when you watch this fun, action packed B-Western from Republic Pictures. There's some great comic relief from Andy Devine and Chester Conklin, two of Hollywood's all-time greats. This old-fashioned shoot-'em-up was directed by William Witney, who would go on to direct many of television's top western sagas, including Bonanza, Wagon Train, and The Wild, Wild West.

programme.title: My Pal Trigger
programme.desc: Roy Rogers goes to jail after hes wrongly accused of killing Gabby Hayes horse in this action packed yarn from Republic Pictures. All of the old jokes about cowboys kissing their horse may have started with this very movie, as it focuses on Roys loving relationship with Trigger, the greatest horse in all of Hollywood. The film also features Roys beloved Dale Evans, plus, adding some country swing to the musical numbers is Roys favorite backing band, the Sons of the Pioneers! Songs include Ole Faithful, Long, Long Ago and Livin Western Style.

programme.title: Boots and Saddles
programme.desc: When a young Englishman inherits a ranch, none other than Gene Autry takes up the challenge of turning the Brit into a real westerner. He convinces the Brit to use the ranch to raise horses, but things quickly grow complicated. Look for the usual cast of characters from Republic Pictures, including Smiley Burnette (as the irresistible Frog Millhouse!). Songs include Take Me Back To My Boots and Saddle, and Ridin the Range.

programme.title: Arizona Bound
programme.desc: The Rough Riders are called upon to help save a stagecoach line. From 1941, starring Buck Jones and Tim McCoy, plus Horace Murphy and Luana Walters. Directed by Spencer Bennett.

programme.title: Wildfire: The Story of a Horse
programme.desc: Fanning (John Miljan) has his men rustle horses and then blame it on a wild horse named Wildfire. Happy and Alkali arrive and immediately get into trouble with Fanning and his men. When Alkali is shot, Happy catches the outlaws but the Judge not only releases them, he discharges the Sheriff and tries to arrest Happy for rustling! Starring Bob Steele and Sterling Holloway, directed by Robert Emmett Tansey. 1945 (Color).

programme.title: Robin Hood of the Pecos
programme.desc: Roy Rogers finds himself elected sheriff in a wild Texas town after the Civil War. But can Roy handle a place under martial law? And can he save Gabby Hayes from being executed? And how many songs will he get to sing? Clear the burrs from your saddle and get ready for a good one, also starring Marjorie Reynolds, plus Sally Payne as Belle Starr, Queen of the Oklahoma Outlaws!

programme.title: The Hanged Man
programme.desc: After a condemned gunfighter (Steve Forrest) survives his own hanging, he helps a young widow stop a pitiless land baron from taking her ranch. This television feature boasts a strong cast of recognizable characters, including Will Geer, Sharon Acker, Dean Jagger, BarBara Luna, and Cameron Mitchell. Director Michael Caffey would go on to work on such hit shows as Barnaby Jones and MacGyver. Also, look for John Mitchum, younger brother of Robert, in a small role. This movie originally aired on ABC-TV and was considered the pilot for a possible series. Writer/producer Andrew Fenady was behind several opular TV shows such as Branded and The Rebel, as well as the 1970 John Wayne feature, Chisum.

programme.title: Buckskin

All Doc has to do is capture the Bridle Brothers - two of most notorious outlaws the West has ever seen. programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly welcome acclaimed Miami chef Michelle Bernstein to a special Miami Beach episode of Chewed Up filmed at the W Hotel in partnership with Experience Miami Beach. After sampling local bakery favorites including guava pastries and tequeños, the hosts celebrate Miami’s vibrant culinary scene and discuss Michelle’s lasting impact on South Florida food culture as she shares stories about her career, mentorship, and being honored during the South Beach Wine & Food Festival’s 25th anniversary celebration. Michelle then teaches the group how to make her signature Cuban sandwich empanadas, transforming the classic Miami staple into a crispy handheld pastry filled with lechón, ham, cheese, pickles, and yellow mustard. As they assemble and fry their empanadas, the conversation ranges from Cuban sandwich authenticity and Latin food traditions to family roots and restaurant life. The group also plays a lively round of “Chew It or Screw It,” debating topics such as late dinner reservations, over-tipping, flip-flops at fine dining restaurants, poolside rosé, and afternoon Cuban coffee. During Fan Mail, they answer viewer questions about cooking with candied jalapeños, discuss which decade they would most like to have grown up in, and share their favorite ways to unwind after a long day. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting Michelle’s empanadas, praising the crisp dough and balanced flavors, while Michelle shares details about her new restaurant, La Cañita. Two audience giveaways are awarded before the hosts encourage viewers to experience everything Miami Beach has to offer.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in a conversation all about meal prepping and healthy breakfast habits while showing Carla Hall and Michael Symon his simple overnight oats recipe. Clinton explains how prepping breakfast the night before has helped him make smarter food choices and stay on track with his health goals, sharing that he has lost 14 pounds through portion control and calorie awareness without giving up the foods he loves. As he combines oats, oat milk, and chia seeds into mason jars, the hosts joke about everything from “wet walnuts” and frozen yogurt nostalgia to chia pets and old-school diet culture. Carla adds her perspective on whole foods and how certain ingredients help keep people full longer, while Michael balances the conversation with humor and practical advice about calories, exercise, and understanding how different foods affect the body. The discussion expands into smart scales, fitness misconceptions, and the importance of focusing on overall wellness instead of obsessing over a single number on the scale. Later in the episode, the hosts answer fan mail questions about making homemade pet food and whether they prefer performing in front of live audiences or taped studio environments, reflecting on their experiences filming at food festivals and live events. The episode wraps with the announcement of the Fan of the Day and sponsor mentions for Fresh Direct and Frontgate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon revisits one of the most important dishes of his career as he makes his famous rotisserie chicken mac and cheese, a creamy pasta recipe that first became a hit at his Cleveland restaurant Piccolo Mondo in 1992. Using rigatoni, heavy cream, goat cheese, fresh rosemary, and shredded rotisserie chicken, Michael walks Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall through the deceptively simple recipe while sharing stories from his early days as a 22-year-old executive chef running a packed live-fire kitchen. The conversation turns nostalgic as Michael explains how the dish followed him from restaurant to restaurant and became such a customer favorite that he eventually had to remove it from the menu. The hosts laugh over Michael’s son Kyle unknowingly eating goat cheese for years despite insisting he hated it, while Clinton passionately defends rosemary after recalling a social media argument about the herb. The episode also features sponsor shout-outs to Fresh Direct and Frontgate, plus playful banter about portion sizes, “fat soup diets,” and airplane etiquette after eating rich pasta dishes. Once plated, the hosts rave about the comforting flavors, especially how the chicken melts into the creamy sauce and how the rosemary balances the tanginess of the goat cheese. The episode wraps with the selection of Melicia from New York as the “Fan of the Day,” followed by more joking between the hosts as they finish off the rich and nostalgic dish.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly leads this Chewed Up Bites episode by transforming leftover rotisserie chicken into a colorful, crunchy chicken salad inspired by the classic Chinese chicken salad popularized by Wolfgang Puck’s Chinois. As he assembles a simple sesame-lime vinaigrette and tosses together cabbage, carrots, bok choy, bell peppers, scallions, and shredded chicken, the hosts discuss the value of leftovers, pantry staples, and the difference between a salad and simply a bowl of lettuce. Michael Symon shares thoughts on menu development and simple comfort foods like peanut butter and jelly sandwiches. The conversation evolves into a broader discussion about healthy eating habits, meal planning, and the realization that not every meal needs to be an indulgent experience. Along the way, the hosts joke about Clinton’s “Gay Soup Diet,” debate the merits of themed ingredient weeks, and exchange playful banter about fashion makeovers, aging, and personal style. Sponsor shout-outs to FreshDirect and Frontgate are woven into the episode, highlighting convenient grocery delivery and entertaining essentials. A fan mail segment sparks more comedy, including the possibility of a future Gay Soup Diet cookbook. The episode wraps with the hosts sampling the finished salad, celebrating its fresh flavors and nutritional balance, before signing off with thanks to viewers and listeners.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this episode of Chewed Up Show, hosts Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon transform rotisserie chicken into Buffalo Chicken Cornbread Melts, the grand finale of Leftover Chicken Week. Carla Hall shares her kitchen genius by combining hot sauce, butter, and shredded rotisserie chicken with a crisp celery blue cheese slaw, served on toasted cornbread rectangles. The hosts discuss ingredient swaps, blue cheese preferences, and techniques for mellowing raw onions. The episode showcases how classic wing flavors elevate simple leftovers into an elegant, less-messy alternative to traditional buffalo wings. The hosts also engage with fan mail, discussing rice rinsing preferences and brainstorming potential spin-off show concepts.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: On Father's Day, Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly are joined by social media cooking sensation Ereka Vetrini to create quick, delicious dishes perfect for summer entertaining. Vetrini, who built a massive following on TikTok and Instagram during the pandemic, shares her approach to accessible Italian-inspired cooking. The episode features two recipes: roasted red pepper bruschetta with Calabrian chili mayo and a perfectly seared 20-minute tuna steak topped with a caper-shallot relish. Between cooking segments, the hosts discuss parenting, picky eaters, navigating social media criticism, and the importance of real food over processed trends. Vetrini shares her philosophy that cooking at home is about making people feel loved and special.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this Father's Day-themed episode of Chewed Up Show, Michael Symon takes the lead in the kitchen preparing grilled pork chops finished with a homemade cherry-bourbon gastrique—a classic French-Italian sweet and sour sauce. Guest host Ereka Vetrini joins returning host Clinton Kelly as they discuss family dining traditions, Michael's father's grilling mishaps, and the importance of proper meat preparation. The episode covers essential cooking tips including salting meat in advance, resting temperatures, and the balance of fat and acid in great sauces. The team also demonstrates the Tumbler knife sharpener and discusses Italian-American family customs around entertaining and basement kitchens.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly welcome culinary producer Allison Cancro as a guest co-host while Carla Hall is performing her one-woman show. The team creates an affordable sausage and peppers dish with corn and tomato salad, keeping the total cost under $25 for four people. Allison shares her Jersey Italian heritage and family food traditions, while the hosts discuss farmer's markets, fresh produce, and regional ingredients. A video segment features Carla Hall preparing bone-in pork chops with smashed sweet potatoes. The episode concludes with a Price Is Right-style pricing game featuring grocery items.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The hosts welcome Jules Esposito, director of food and beverage operations for the Charlie Palmer Collective and Appalachian Hotels, to celebrate summer entertaining. Jules shares her Stone Fruit Panzanella recipe—a bread salad featuring stone fruits, tomatoes, fresh herbs, and a white balsamic vinaigrette that's ideal for casual gatherings. The episode explores Jules's journey from event planning in Paris to her current role overseeing operations for Charlie Palmer's restaurant empire, plus spirited discussions about seasonal eating, herb gardens, pizza regionalism, and the art of hosting.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly kick off July 4th week with guest Jeanette Donnarumma, a returning favorite from the Rachael Ray Show who pioneered the "Watermelon Palooza" segment. Together they prepare summer cookout essentials: Watermelon Aperol Margaritas with frozen fruit ice, a Watermelon Cucumber Salad served in an artfully carved watermelon bowl, and taste-test limited-edition Lay's World Cup chips. The episode celebrates simple, accessible recipes perfect for backyard gatherings, with Donnarumma sharing pro tips on watermelon carving and ingredient selection while the hosts discuss recipe inspiration and kitchen mood-boosters.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon joins Clinton Kelly and Stacy London for a July Fourth-themed episode where he teaches the notoriously non-cooking Stacy how to grill skirt steak with a quick charred corn salsa featuring tomatillos, jalapeños, and garlic. The episode showcases fundamental cooking techniques including seasoning, temperature control, and proper resting. Stacy surprises the group with an air fryer and attempts to make dairy-free s'mores using gluten-free cookies and specialty peanut butter cups, leading to comedic mishaps. The hosts also discuss their new show "Why'd They Wear That," where they critique character wardrobes from films and television. The episode emphasizes that cooking is about patience, proper technique, and feeding people you care about.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly welcome back chef Ariane Duarte for an episode focused on effortless summer cooking. Duarte demonstrates her signature one-pan scallop recipe featuring Jersey cherry tomatoes and fresh corn, emphasizing technique-forward but quick preparation. She shares pro tips on drying scallops for proper searing, building flavor through layered seasoning, and understanding the importance of pan temperature. The hosts discuss the versatility of the dish, noting it can be adapted with shrimp, snapper, halibut, or local New Jersey fish like fluke or stripers. Between cooking segments, the trio play a "This or That" game and discuss meaningful conversations with strangers, touching on mindfulness and the importance of slowing down.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this summer picnic episode, Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly are joined by celebrated chef Ayesha Nurdjaja to explore classic picnic fare with cultural roots. Ayesha shares her family's cherished recipe for a Potato Egg Provolone Semolina Hero, a humble yet satisfying dish that speaks to her Brooklyn heritage and Italian-Indonesian background. The hosts discuss picnic traditions, sandwich preferences, and the art of making the perfect egg dish. The episode includes a blind taste-identification game with picnic basket items and closes with fan mail discussing induction stoves, overhyped foods, and emerging culinary talents to watch.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up Show dives into world-class dips with hosts Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly, joined by guest Ayesha Nurdjaja. Clinton prepares a simple homemade tzatziki with Greek yogurt, cucumber, garlic, and fresh herbs, discussing how it's become a healthy staple in his household. Carla brings a grilled corn dip featuring mayo, sour cream, scallions, cilantro, and charred corn. Michael shares a Cleveland clam dip recipe—a regional specialty featuring diced clams, bacon, dill, and sour cream. The hosts discuss dip culture, texture preferences, and creative variations like using kohlrabi and roasted beets. Between recipes, they play never-have-I-ever games and answer fan questions about dessert preferences and vacation spots.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: You asked, you receive! Welcome to a Mailbag episode. Dave and Chris answer the questions that listeners and viewers like YOU sent in. From what "having a perspective" looks like as a restaurant to Dave's go-to hot sauce to must-own cookware, the pair cover a ton of topics.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by resident young people Kelly and Charlotte to see if he's in touch with the youth. The trio play a new version of inter-generational "Never Have I Ever" with jelly bean punishments. They then discuss dining habits of the younger generations and how they have changed since Dave came up in the industry. Dave finishes with a summer-snack-themed Ask Dave.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Brandon Jew's "MSG" Fried Chicken" - Chef Brandon Jew, owner of Michelin-starred Mr. Jiu's in San Francisco's Chinatown, shares his recipe and techniques for making his "MSG" (mushroom/seaweed/garlic) Fried Chicken, inspired by the original fried chicken recipe from his grandfather's restaurant, The Four Seas.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Nico de Leon's Inasal Fried Chicken Sandwich" - Chef Nico de Leon shares his recipe for a delicious twice fried Inasal Chicken Sandwich, bringing in Filipino flavors and plenty of pro tips for creating a beautiful sandwich.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Invitation to a Banquet: A Conversation with Fuchsia Dunlop" - Step into the vibrant world of Chinese cuisine with acclaimed author and culinary adventurer, Fuchsia Dunlop, as she unveils the flavors and stories behind her latest book, "Invitation to a Banquet." Dunlop stops by the Majordomo Media studio to chat with Chris Ying to discuss her lastest literary achivement and some perspectives from her 30+ years of studying Sichuan food and Chinese cuisine.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Pork and Chive Dumplings with Chris Ying" - Chris Ying has made a lot of dumplings in his day. In fact, he always keeps a batch in the freezer for whenever the kids need a quick dinner. Today, he shares his go-to pork dumpling recipe, including two quick sauces.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: The Pinocchio - Sing Along With Tobee - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Caitie made a fluffy bird marionette named Fifi! Fifi is full of jokes and Tobee can't stop laughing! Caitie knows another marionette who is just as silly as Fifi. It's Pinocchio! There's even a whole song about him called "The Pinocchio." After Caitie and Tobee sing and dance to the song, Fifi can't help but tell one last joke. And guess what? It quacks Tobee up!

programme.title: Little Billy's Skateboard - Carl's Car Wash and more
programme.desc: The Bubblegum Bandit pulls another bubblegum burglary and is about to escape when Officer Oona bursts his bubble! Moments later, little Billy skates right into the action and his skateboard becomes a gummy mess! Billy hurries to Carl’s Car Wash, but Carl won't clean his skateboard - it's a CAR wash, not a SKATEBOARD wash! Billy does some quick thinking...and his skateboard might just go through the car wash after all!

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Animals
programme.desc: This is all about Animals! Includes children's favorites “Alice the Camel”, “Let’s Go To Zoo” and More!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Space & Planets
programme.desc: This compilation is about Space & Planets! Includes children's songs "Mr. Golden Sun", “Skidamarink” & "More!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Underwater
programme.desc: This compilation is about Underwater! Includes children's songs "Baby Shark", "Sailor Went To Sea" and More!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Counting and Numbers
programme.desc: The Super Simple show is a compilation of Super Simple’s most popular songs and series that our fans love! This compilation is all about Numbers and Counting! Includes children's song favorites "10 Little Buses", “10 In the Bed” & "10 Little Dinosaurs" and featuring Carl's Car Wash, The Bumble Nums, Sing Along With Tobee, and More!

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the exciting world of shapes and colors! Learn about the primary colors red, yellow, and blue with real-life imagery of animals and objects. Explore colors like purple, orange, and green and see artwork by Van Gogh! Discover the circles, squares, and triangles that surround us in everyday life, and introduce baby to the world of visual arts as children and artists create their own work!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Early on in babies' development, they begin to recognize primary colors such as blue, red, and yellow. And all the while, they're also processing an infinite variety of new sights and sounds. Think of the joy you'll experience as you introduce your little one to beautiful music and imagery with Baby Beethoven Symphony of Fun.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Share a rainbow of sights and sounds with your baby as you discover the wonders of color together! Join puppet host "Vincent Van Goat" as he creates six Van Gogh masterpieces exploring red, green, blue, yellow, orange, and purple. World Of Colors is a spirited celebration of creative expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Bong Dong Kids Trucks Adventure
programme.desc: Join the police cars Bong & Dong fight Mobster Moby along with other transport like garbage trucks, fire engine, ambulance, crane,loader, tractor, digger, driller, backhoe loader, sports cars and more. This kids show helps child learn the abc, numbers, shapes, colors.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When an undersea world is threatened by Captain Memo and his lobster sub, Sonic spreads a rumor that Memo plans to attack Robotnik next.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sonic and The Freedom Fighters take out Robotnik's main computer, they're trapped by Swat-Bots in an underground tunnel, only to be rescued by a handsome teenager named Griff. Griff takes them deep beneath the Big City.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik severely upsets the ecosystem above and below the surface, when he sends explosive, Robotnik snake probes drilling deep under ground in search of oil lakes.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic reluctantly takes Tails with him to the Big City to find some cotter pins for the Freedom Fighters catapults. While in the City, Sonic is nearly eaten alive by his old dog Muttski, now a roboti-sized guard dog in the Junk Heaps

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters run into a Swashbuckler, named Ari, battling Swat-Bots. Sonic diverts them, allowing Ari to escape. After hearing that Robotnik is holding his band of Freedom Fighters hostage, Ari decides to help Sonic free them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs steal historical artifacts of Mobuis from Robotnik before he can pawn them off at an auction.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A group of Freedom Fighters is obliterated by a swarm of metallic fly-bots. When Manic goes looking for the insects, he is stung with serum.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs congregate on the Floating Island in their latest effort to find their mother. But Sleet and Dingo also have an interest in this island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: As Sonic begins to chow chili dogs for a snack, he finds a message in the sack from his mother urging him to head to Speedster Island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic becomes aggravated that maybe their efforts of fighting for freedom seem pointless. Sonia suggests that they go to the Oracle for advice.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik has his eyes set upon Queen Aleena's crown jewel, but Sonic, Manic and Sonia plan to retrieve the gem before Robotnik.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell, Ellie, and Kiki are looking hard for something. It turns out that when Thunderbell was trying on Kiki's crown, he accidentally dropped it and a gem went missing. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. Rainbow Ruby designs hats where the birds can comfortably sit without disturbing the villagers. // The village friends were planning to have some fun with the rainbow fish but they're all disappointed to discover that the lake has frozen over.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is gathered around to watch the last snowberrykin melt. As the last bit of snow forms a puddle, a cheer goes up, spring is here! It's now time for the annual BerryFest. But with Princess Berrykin out of town on business, who will be the BerryFest Princess and preside over the festival?! Everyone is abuzz with how to go about choosing the Princess and what her duties will be. Postmaster Bee declares the only fair way to go about it is to hold an election. Everyone agrees.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Overhearing Blueberry reading a story to the baby Berrykins about the scary Galumphalot, Orange is uncharacteristically shaken. She determines she doesn't want anything scary around her, just relaxation. But while the girls are having a hair-styling party at Lemon's salon, a malfunction at the BerryWorks causes the whole town to go dark. And Orange begins to get really anxious.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Blueberry has become obsessed with the mystery book detective heroine, Patti Persimmon. She decorates her store with Patti posters and rearranges her shelves to make room for all the Patti books she's ordered. Blueberry begins to neglect outings with her friends and even goes so far as to dress like her idol just to emulate her. She then starts looking for cases to solve around town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The grove around Berry Bitty City is abuzz with activity. The Berrykins are using their sparkles to harvest berries, Berrykin Bloom is in his garden spraying flowers with a special solution to make them larger, and Raspberry is nearby gathering flower petals to make perfume. The wind starts to blow and picks up some of the sparkles. The sparkles float along the wind and get combined with some of Berrykin Bloom's solution.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The girls are all gathered at the café. Strawberry is experimenting making applesauce from scratch with a mallet (and making a mess) while the rest busily make their own wrapping paper and bows for the annual gift exchange they hold in celebration of First Frost. Mr. Longface stops by and Strawberry offers him some fresh applesauce but he declines, as he doesn't care for it. He's only there for some tea and, being that he's new in town, doesn't know what the group is up to.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Raspberry is distraught when she suspects her friends have copied her design on her lantern for the "Glimmerberry Gathering".

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While sipping cider in Strawberry's café, the girls muse about how they cannot wait for winter to arrive. They list the various traditions they love about the season but the one they all agree on is the Glimmerberry Ball. Strawberry mentions that she doesn't think the café can hold the party this year because of how the town has grown. The girls decide to split up and search for a new location.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While working in her store, Orange overhears the Berrykins laughing and shouting outside. When she goes to the door to investigate, a clump of dandelion fluff gets blown inside chased by three Berrykins. She watches as they head butt the clump around until it floats back toward Orange. Just as it floats close, she gives it a bat sending it outside and the Berrykins happily chasing after it. Wanting in on the fun, she joins them.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While enjoying lunch in the café, the girls are interrupted by some music coming from the gazebo. They head outside and find Sadiebug and Kadiebug wearing badges and standing by a banner and podium. A group of chairs are set up in front of them. The twins begin to announce, and then argue, that they are starting a club - a good citizens club. The girls are shocked as the twins argue with each other all the time.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With all the Baby Berrykins running around Berry Bitty City, Princess Berrykin decides to build a Day Care Center and playground. But she's worried because it's dull-looking - all grey metal and brown wood. She asks Strawberry whom might be good to help her out. And two names immediately come to her mind - Lemon and Raspberry! The Princess confesses she's always admired their sense of design and style.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: After returning to the café after a walk with her pets, Strawberry is met by Mr. Longface. He's been waiting for her so that they can get started making treats and decorating for Sadiebug's and Kadiebug's party that afternoon. But Custard and Pupcake have enjoyed their time outside so much that they want to continue playing.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest; she wants to fit in and contribute to the community.

programme.title: War Journal: The Incredible World War II Escape of Major Damon Rocky Gause
programme.desc: The thrilling true story of a nearly impossible escape from prison camp during World War II. In 1942, American airman Rocky Gause escaped a prison camp on the Bataan Peninsula, swam to nearby Corregidor Island, and then sailed with U.S. serviceman William Osborne in a leaky fishing boat 3,200 miles from the Philippines to Australia. Despite facing typhoons, constant threats from Japanese ships, submarines and airplanes, a lack of water and food, and even a visit to the worlds largest leper colony, both men survived to recount their harrowing 52-day ordeal. Filmed in Bataan, Corregidor, and in Manila, the documentary features the journals of Gause and Osborne, archival footage and photos, and interviews with relatives and Army associates. It is narrated by three-time Emmy Award-winning actor Chandler Massey, and actor Kyle Chandler (Argo, Zero Dark Thirty and Carol) provides the voice of Rocky.

programme.title: The Portillo Expedition: Mystery on the Bougainville Island
programme.desc: THE PORTILLO EXPEDITION: MYSTERY ON BOUGAINVILLE ISLAND examines the circumstances surrounding the death of Isoroku Yamamoto the Japanese WWII Admiral who planned the Pearl Harbor surprise attack and the Battle at Midway. Admiral Yamamotos plane, the Betty Bomber, was shot down by American P-38 fighter planes in 1943. Narrated by Gary Sinise, the documentary follows a crew of American explorers to the remote jungle of the western Solomon Islands, where they search for the wreck of the Betty Bomber sparking a further quest to understand what they find there.
